apply.title: Apply {{n}} changes?
apply.inplaceDesc: Directly overwrite the original session file
apply.inplaceFootnote: A snapshot is auto-created before applying and can be restored at any time; if verification fails it auto-reverts, leaving no changes.
apply.saveasFootnote: The original session stays unchanged; the result is saved as a new session.

snapRestore.title: Restore to this snapshot?
snapRestore.desc: Session "{{title}}" will be restored to the state at {{time}}.
snapRestore.bullet1: Changes to the current session after this snapshot will be overwritten.
snapRestore.bullet2: Ferry will auto-create a protective snapshot of the current state before restoring.
snapRestore.bullet3: After restore, you can undo this operation via that protective snapshot.
snapRestore.bullet4: Other sessions in the source tool are not affected.

delete.title: Delete this session?
delete.desc: Session "{{title}}" ({{tool}}) will be removed from this machine.
delete.bulletSub: This session contains {{n}} sub-sessions, which will also be deleted.
delete.bulletSnapshot: A snapshot is auto-saved to "Snapshots & Restore" before deletion.
delete.bulletOpenCode: OpenCode sessions cannot be undone after deletion; only the exported snapshot is kept.
delete.bulletUndoable: After deletion, you can undo via the toast or the snapshots page.
delete.bulletBatchSnapshot: Each session is auto-snapshotted to "Snapshots & Restore" before deletion.
delete.bulletBatchOpenCode: {{n}} of them are OpenCode sessions that cannot be undone after deletion.
delete.bulletBatchRest: Other sessions can be restored one by one on the snapshots page.
